Q14. Statements: 
Only a few Day are Night.
All Night are Noon.
Conclusion
I: All Day can be Noon.
II: Some noon is day.
q:
Directions (1417): In each of the questions below some statements are given followed by two conclusions. You have to take the given statements to be true even if they seem to be at variance with commonly known facts. Read all the conclusions and then decide which of the given conclusions logically follows from the given statements disregarding commonly known facts.
-
Q14. Statements: 
Only a few Day are Night.
All Night are Noon.
Conclusion
I: All Day can be Noon.
II: Some noon is day.

options.text: If either conclusion I or II follows.
If neither conclusion I nor II follows.
If both conclusions I and II follow.

explanation: I: The restriction only stops Day from sitting entirely inside Night. It can sit inside Noon (True). II: Since all Night is Noon and some Day is Night, some Day is also Noon (True). Both follow.

Statements: M ->- U ->- L -≤- N;- L -≥- Y ->- A
Conclusions: 
I. Y -<- N
$II. Y  =  N$
q:
Statements: M ->- U ->- L -≤- N;- L -≥- Y ->- A
Conclusions: 
I. Y -<- N
$II. Y  =  N$

options.text: Either conclusion I or II is true
Both conclusions I and II are true
Neither conclusion I nor II is true

explanation: $Since Y -≤- L -≤- N, we get Y -≤- N. This means either Y -<- N or Y = N is true$.

Conclusions: 
I. M -<- J
II. J ->- L
q:
$Statements: J -≥- A ->- D  =  E;- L -<- D -<- M$
Conclusions: 
I. M -<- J
II. J ->- L

options.text: Either conclusion I or II is true
Both conclusions I and II are true
Neither conclusion I nor II is true

explanation: I: M and J cannot be compared (False). II: J -≥- A ->- D ->- L implies J ->- L (True). Only II is true.

subject: Quantitative Aptitude
question: Present age of Deepa is 50% more than that of Shiva and age of Deepa 5 years ago was 50% of the age of Shiva 10 years hence. Find sum of their age 5 years later?
q: Present age of Deepa is 50% more than that of Shiva and age of Deepa 5 years ago was 50% of the age of Shiva 10 years hence. Find sum of their age 5 years later?
explanation: $Let Shiva = 2x, Deepa = 3x. 3x 5 = 0.5 (2x + 10) ⇒ x = 5. Present ages: Shiva = 10, Deepa = 15. Sum 5 years later = 15 + 20 = 35 years$.

question: Two types of rice which cost Rs. 40/kg and Rs. 60/kg are mixed and marked at Rs. \frac{200}{3} Kg. If shopkeeper earns 25% profit after allow a discount of 10% on mixture, then find in what respective ratio these two types of rice are mixed?
q: Two types of rice which cost Rs. 40/kg and Rs. 60/kg are mixed and marked at Rs. \frac{200}{3} Kg. If shopkeeper earns 25% profit after allow a discount of 10% on mixture, then find in what respective ratio these two types of rice are mixed?
explanation: $MP = \frac{200}{3}. SP = 0.9 \frac{200}{3} = 60. CP = 60 ÷ 1.25 = 48. Allegation between 40 and 60 with mean 48 gives ratio (6048):(4840) = 12:8 = 3:2$.

question: To complete a work, A works for 15 days -&- B works for 8 days. It can also be completed when they together work for 12 days. Find the difference between wages received by them if A works for 9 days -&- B works for 16 days on the same work and total wages received for the work is Rs. 4200.
q: To complete a work, A works for 15 days -&- B works for 8 days. It can also be completed when they together work for 12 days. Find the difference between wages received by them if A works for 9 days -&- B works for 16 days on the same work and total wages received for the work is Rs. 4200.
explanation: $15A + 8B = 12A + 12B ⇒ 3A = 4B ⇒ A/B = \frac{4}{3}. Total work = 84. Work done by A = 36, B = 48. Wage ratio = 36:48 = 3:4. Wages: A=1800, B=2400. Diff = Rs. 600$.

question: If ratio between total surface area of cylinder to its curved surface area is $4:3$ then radius of cylinder is what percent less then height of cylinder?
q: If ratio between total surface area of cylinder to its curved surface area is 4:3 then radius of cylinder is what percent less then height of cylinder?
explanation: $TSA/CSA = (r+h)/h = \frac{4}{3} ⇒ 3r+3h = 4h ⇒ h = 3r ⇒ r = h/3. Radius is 66_\frac{2}{3} -\%- less than height$.

question: $The average age of Satish, Sandy -&- Abhi is 32 years and the ratio of their ages 10 years ago was 2:4 -: 5 respectively -. Find the present age of Satish?$
q: The average age of Satish, Sandy -&- Abhi is 32 years and the ratio of their ages 10 years ago was 2:4 -: 5 respectively -. Find the present age of Satish?
explanation: $Sum \text{ of } present ages = 96. Sum 10 years ago = 66. Parts: 2+4+5 = 11 parts = 66 ⇒ 1 part = 6. Satish 10 yrs ago = 12. Present = 22 years$.

q: Ten men or 8 women can complete a piece of work in 4 days and 5 days respectively. Find in how many days 2 men and 3 women together can complete the same piece of work.
explanation: $40 Mdays = 40 Wdays ⇒ 1M = 1W. Total work = 40. 2M + 3W = 5 workers. Time = 40 ÷ 5 = 8 days$.

question: A train running at the speed of 144 km/hr crosses a man who is running at the speed of 18 km/hr in opposite direction of train in 8 sec. Find time taken by train to cross a platform, whose length is 66_\frac{2}{3} -%- more than length of train?
q: A train running at the speed of 144 km/hr crosses a man who is running at the speed of 18 km/hr in opposite direction of train in 8 sec. Find time taken by train to cross a platform, whose length is 66_\frac{2}{3} -%- more than length of train?
explanation: $Relative speed = (144+18) \frac{5}{18} = 45 m/s. Train length L = 45 × 8 = 360m. Platform length = 360 + 240 = 600m. Total distance = 960m. Speed = 40 m/s. Time = \frac{960}{40} = 24 seconds$.

question: Speed of boat in still water is twice the speed of stream and difference between upstream and downstream speed of boat is 8 km/hours. Find total time taken by boat to cover 48 km distance in downstream and 32 km distance in upstream?
q: Speed of boat in still water is twice the speed of stream and difference between upstream and downstream speed of boat is 8 km/hours. Find total time taken by boat to cover 48 km distance in downstream and 32 km distance in upstream?
explanation: $Downstream speed = 3s, Upstream = s. Diff = 2s = 8 ⇒ s = 4 km/hr. Still speed = 8. Downstream = 12, Upstream = 4. Time = \frac{48}{12} + \frac{32}{4} = 4 + 8 = 12 hours$.

question: In a vessel quantity of water is 40% of quantity of milk. If 42 liter of mixture is taken out from the vessel and 32 liter of water is added, then new ratio of milk to water in vessel becomes $7:6$. Find initial quantity of mixture in vessel?
q: In a vessel quantity of water is 40% of quantity of milk. If 42 liter of mixture is taken out from the vessel and 32 liter of water is added, then new ratio of milk to water in vessel becomes 7:6. Find initial quantity of mixture in vessel?
explanation: $Water = 40\% \text{ of } Milk ⇒ Milk:Water = 5:2. Out \text{ of } 42L: Milk removed = 30L, Water removed = 12L. (5x30) ÷ (2x12+32) = \frac{7}{6} ⇒ 30x 180 = 14x + 140 ⇒ 16x = 320 ⇒ x = 20. Initial = 7x = 140 liters$.
